title: AutoBe benchmark: structured harness narrows frontier-vs-local gap in backend generation [D]
author: Reddit r/MachineLearning
summary: AutoBe is a novel, statically scored benchmark for end-to-end backend generation that enforces structured function-calling to produce six deterministic artifacts (e.g., ERD, OpenAPI spec, NestJS code) via AST filling—not free-form code generation. It reveals tight score clustering across models, showing local and cost-efficient models (e.g., Qwen3.5-27B) rivaling frontier LLMs when the harness is well-structured—highlighting harness design over model scale as the primary quality bottleneck. The benchmark emphasizes reproducibility, compile-time correctness, and cost-aware evaluation, with explicit caveats about generalization beyond procedural, reference-guided tasks.
